title: Anime mythology lecture and workshop in London incl Eva
display_title: Anime mythology lecture and workshop in London incl Eva
wikitext: []: Anime mythology lecture and workshop in London (incl. Eva)             Quote Post by AdamDobay » Sat Sep 21, 2013 7:21 pm Hello everyone, I'd like to invite those of you who live or currently reside in the UK to an anime lecture and workshop, both free events to be held at the Queen Mary University of London . Since Evangelion will be involved, I hope you don't mind me signing on to the forum mainly to impart the following information. In short, I'm an anime scholar involved with film analysis, mythology, psychotherapy and Eastern spirituality. I've been researching anime storytelling for the past couple of years, but my work has been confined to my native Hungary up until now. This is the first time I'll be presenting my research in English. The lecture, entitled Mythological Storytelling in Anime , will take place on Wednesday 25 September 2013 from 18.00 to 20.00 , where I will elaborate on my hunches about how mythic thinking permeates much of anime, how therapeutic processes are incorporated into fantastical stories. This will lay down the groundwork for the deep dive on Saturday. At the Advanced Anime Analysis workshop on Saturday 28 September from 10.00 to 13.30 , I have decided to tackle topics that my audience there is interested in. Half of those who signed up so far have written that they are interested in shoujo mythologies from the emancipation of the Sacred Feminine to the shift in gender roles that reverberate in the magical girl motif; the other half wants to hear about shounen and seinen mythologies from the wandering priest theme to the deconstruction of the male role and the global/personal apocalypse that this is correlated with. So I've decided to do both. Since a complete reading of Evangelion would be impossible in such a timeframe (I did a scene-by-scene analysis once with my anime study group, that took about 25 hours if I recall), I will start out at the first episode of the show and how its events can be paralleled with initiation rites, how the show mythologizes puberty, how it uses Western symbols to paraphrase Eastern concept, and how the show's creators construct scenes that can have three or four layers of interpretation which can run parallel without interfering with one another. As Evangelion is one of the most discussed and dissected anime ever made, I must point out in advance that mine, too, will be one interpretation amongs the vast sea of possible interpretations, so what I hope to give is a number of viewpoints and perspectives that the show can be approached with when viewed. Those interested based on the above are welcome to attend both the lecture and seminar. Please note that because of an attendance cap of 30 persons per event because of our room size, I've created a registration form that enables us to track how many people we can let in. It takes about one minute to fill. Don't worry, there are still plenty of spaces at this time. For those who fall outside the vicinity of the events, there will be a video recording of both occasions which will be edited and put online at a later date. For any questions regarding the above please don't hesitate to ask. Regards, Adam Dobay
